title: astragalus flexuosus var. fendleri
intro: Astragalus flexuosus var. fendleri, also known as Fendler's milkvetch or Fendler's astragalus, is a type of perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that have been traditionally used to boost the immune system and protect against infections. The health benefits of Astragalus flexuosus var. fendleri include reducing stress and fatigue, improving respiratory function, and enhancing overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the root of the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture, or combined with other herbs to create a supplement that supports immune function and promotes vitality.
